engineGuide.h2: Toyota Regius Ace Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Toyota Regius Ace across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Common-rail turbo diesel introduced in H200 Regius Ace; powerful but prone to injector seal carbon buildup known as 'black death'.
commonFailures: Injector seal failure causing carbon buildup ('black death') - typically 80k-120k miles
VNT turbo actuator sticking - high mileage units
EGR valve clogging leading to rough idle and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1KD-FTV Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Indirect injection diesel powering early LH series Regius Ace; robust but requires strict timing belt maintenance on interference design.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing catastrophic valve damage - replace every 60k miles
Injector pump wear leading to hard starting and smoke
Head gasket failure on overheated units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 5L Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Smaller indirect injection diesel for base LH series models; simpler design but shares timing belt vulnerability with 5L.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure (interference engine) - critical replacement interval
Glow plug circuit failures causing cold start issues
Fuel injector wear on high-mileage commercial use
entries.cta: Get quotes for 3L Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level diesel for early Regius Ace; basic indirect injection design favoured for simplicity and low-cost repairs.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure risk (interference design)
Injector pump calibration drift causing smoke
Cooling system failures leading to head warp
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2L Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: Modern VVT-i petrol engine for H200 Regius Ace; reliable but distributor ignition on early units can cause no-start faults.
commonFailures: Ignition coil/distributor failure causing misfires - 70k+ miles
VVT-i actuator rattle on cold start
Oil consumption on high-mileage commercial use
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2TR-FE Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Smaller VVT-i petrol option for H200 Regius Ace; efficient but less common in UK grey imports.
commonFailures: VVT-i actuator failure causing timing faults
Ignition coil pack failures on high-mileage units
Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection variants)
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1TR-FE Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: Distributor-ignition petrol engine for RCH series Regius Ace; simple design but distributor failures are common failure point.
commonFailures: Distributor ignition failure causing no-spark condition
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to rattling noise
Oil leaks from rear main seal on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 3RZ-FE Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Toyota Regius Ace Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Toyota Regius Ace relies on proven Toyota commercial engines, but high-mileage grey imports often face injector seal carbon buildup on 1KD-FTV diesels and timing belt failures on LH series 5L/3L units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific variant.

problems.h4: 1KD-FTV Injector Seal Failure ('Black Death') - Why H200 Regius Ace Diesels Fail
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Regius Ace KDH206V, KDH221K, KDH223B (2005-2017, 1KD-FTV 3.0L D4D)
problems.rootCause: Injector copper washers degrade, allowing combustion gases to carbonise around injectors, causing ticking noise and eventual seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals/washers, clean carbon deposits
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before cylinder head damage; preventative maintenance recommended at 60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, decarbonising, valve inspection, injector seat machining
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if carbon damage limited to head; not viable if block scored

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt long block with upgraded seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for commercial vehicles; eliminates repeat failure risk, minimal downtime

problems.recommendation: On a Regius Ace worth £3,000-£8,000, injector seal replacement is cost-effective if caught at first symptom (ticking noise). If carbon has scored the block or head, a rebuilt engine with upgraded seals offers better long-term value and faster turnaround for revenue-generating vans.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1KD-FTV engine prices for your Toyota Regius Ace

problems.h4: 5L Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Risk on LH Series Regius Ace
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Regius Ace LH113V, LH178V, LH186B (1995-2004, 5L 3.0L diesel)
problems.rootCause: The 5L is an interference engine; a snapped timing belt causes pistons to strike valves, bending both and destroying the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idler pulleys, water pump
repairOptions.longevity: Critical maintenance item; must be done every 60k miles or 5 years regardless of symptoms

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new belt kit fitted, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after belt failure; rebuilt unit with upgraded components prevents repeat catastrophic failure

problems.recommendation: There is no intermediate repair after a 5L timing belt snaps - the engine is destroyed. For LH series Regius Ace owners, preventative belt replacement is essential. If failure has occurred, a rebuilt engine is more cost-effective than attempting valve/piston repairs on a commercial vehicle.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 5L Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement

problems.h4: 1KD-FTV Turbocharger & EGR Failures - Loss of Power on High-Mileage Units
problems.rootCause: VNT turbo vanes stick from carbon buildup; EGR valve clogs from recirculated soot, causing limp mode and reduced boost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean/replace turbo actuator, EGR valve, clean intake manifold
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo housing intact; EGR blanking may prevent recurrence but check emissions compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo/EGR components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for commercial fleets needing reliability; eliminates multiple failure points in one replacement

problems.recommendation: If turbo/EGR issues are isolated and the engine block is healthy, cleaning/replacement can restore performance. However, on high-mileage commercial Regius Ace vans where downtime costs money, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components often provides better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace 1KD-FTV replacement engine prices

problems.h4: 3RZ-FE Distributor Ignition Failure - No-Start Fault on RCH Series
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Regius Ace RCH41W, RCH42W (1999-2005, 3RZ-FE 2.7L petrol)
problems.rootCause: Distributor cap/rotor wear or ignition coil failure prevents spark generation, causing cranks-but-no-start symptoms.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace distributor cap, rotor arm, ignition coil, HT leads
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective fix if engine otherwise healthy; parts readily available via HiAce compatibility

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new ignition components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has additional high-mileage wear (oil consumption, compression loss)

problems.recommendation: Distributor failures are usually isolated and inexpensive to fix. Only consider full engine replacement if the 3RZ-FE also shows signs of high-mileage wear like oil burning or low compression - otherwise, repair the ignition system and maintain with regular distributor service.
problems.cta: Check rebuilt 3RZ-FE engine availability for your Toyota Regius Ace

problems.h4: Diesel Starting Problems - Glow Plug & Injector Pump Issues on LH Series
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Regius Ace LH113V, LH178V (1995-2004, 5L/3L/2L diesel)
problems.rootCause: Glow plug circuit failure prevents cold starting; injector pump wear reduces fuel pressure causing hard starting when warm.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Test/replace glow plugs, relay, wiring harness connections
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix for cold-start issues; genuine Toyota plugs recommended for longevity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, bench-test and recalibrate or replace diesel injection pump
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if pump wear confirmed; rebuilt pumps available with warranty

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new pump fitted, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has multiple high-mileage issues beyond just starting problems

problems.recommendation: Start with glow plug diagnosis for cold-start issues - it's inexpensive and often the culprit. If the engine starts fine when warm but struggles when hot, suspect injector pump wear. For commercial LH series Regius Ace vans with 100k+ miles, a rebuilt engine may offer better reliability than multiple component repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for LH series Toyota Regius Ace diesel engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Toyota Regius Ace
finalCta.paragraph: From injector seal carbon on 1KD-FTV diesels to timing belt catastrophes on LH series 5L engines, Regius Ace owners face specific, well-documented failure patterns. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Toyota Regius Ace specialists who understand grey import commercial vans. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get your revenue-generating van back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Toyota Regius Ace Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Toyota Regius Ace and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Toyota Regius Ace Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Toyota Regius Ace is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

groups.title: Toyota Regius Ace Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Toyota Regius Ace 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.4L 2L indirect injection diesel powered base model LH series Regius Ace vans from 1995-2004. A simple, robust design favoured for low-cost commercial operation, though timing belt maintenance is critical on this interference engine.
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Regius Ace 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Regius Ace 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L 3L indirect injection diesel offered a balance of economy and torque for mid-spec LH series Regius Ace vans. Shares the 5L's interference design and timing belt vulnerability but with slightly lower output.
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Regius Ace 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Regius Ace 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L displacement covers both the indirect injection 5L (LH series) and common-rail turbo 1KD-FTV (H200). The 1KD-FTV dominates UK replacement demand due to injector seal 'black death' issues on high-mileage grey imports.
commonFailurePoints: 5L: timing belt snapped; 1KD-FTV: injector seal carbon buildup
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Regius Ace 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Toyota Regius Ace Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Toyota Regius Ace 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L 1TR-FE VVT-i petrol engine appeared in later H200 Regius Ace variants. Less common in UK grey imports but offers smoother operation and lower emissions than diesel equivalents for urban commercial use.
items.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Regius Ace 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.7L displacement covers both the distributor-ignition 3RZ-FE (RCH series) and modern VVT-i 2TR-FE (H200). The 2TR-FE is more common in UK imports; both suffer ignition component failures at high mileage.
commonFailurePoints: 3RZ-FE: distributor ignition failure; 2TR-FE: ignition coil failure
items.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace 2.7L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Toyota Regius Ace Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Toyota Regius Ace engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early LH series diesels to the latest H200 D4D units.

items.title: Toyota Regius Ace Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines dominate the Regius Ace range, with indirect injection 2L/3L/5L units in LH series (1995-2004) and common-rail turbo 1KD-FTV in H200 (2005-2017) driving most UK replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominate the Regius Ace range, with indirect injection 2L/3L/5L units in LH series (1995-2004) and common-rail turbo 1KD-FTV in H200 (2005-2017) driving most UK replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota Regius Ace Diesel Engine Families
families: 1KD-FTV - H200 common-rail turbo diesel (KDH206V/KDH223B), injector seal 'black death' risk
5L - LH series indirect injection (LH178V), catastrophic timing belt failure if neglected
3L - LH series smaller diesel (LH113V), shares timing belt vulnerability with 5L
2L - Base LH series diesel, simple design but interference engine
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for commercial van loading
1KD-FTV injector seal carbon buildup at 80k-120k miles
LH series timing belt critical maintenance every 60k miles
HiAce parts compatibility simplifies sourcing for grey imports
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Regius Ace Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Toyota Regius Ace Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Regius Ace variants use robust Toyota VVT-i engines (1TR-FE, 2TR-FE) or older distributor-ignition 3RZ-FE units, offering smoother operation but higher fuel consumption than diesel equivalents.
items.descriptor: Petrol Regius Ace variants use robust Toyota VVT-i engines (1TR-FE, 2TR-FE) or older distributor-ignition 3RZ-FE units, offering smoother operation but higher fuel consumption than diesel equivalents.
items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota Regius Ace Petrol Engine Families
families: 2TR-FE - H200 VVT-i petrol (TRH200K), reliable but ignition coil failures at high mileage
1TR-FE - Later H200 smaller VVT-i (KDH200K), less common in UK imports
3RZ-FE - RCH series distributor ignition (RCH41W), distributor failure common fault
knownFor: Smoother, quieter operation than diesel for urban commercial use
Ignition component failures (coils, distributors) at 70k+ miles
Lower torque than diesel equivalents affects laden performance
Generally lower replacement demand than diesel Regius Ace variants

engineYears.h2: Toyota Regius Ace Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Toyota Regius Ace production run (1995-2017), organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: LH Series Launch: Indirect Injection Diesels Debut
years.description: The Regius Ace launched on the LH platform with simple indirect injection diesel engines designed for commercial durability and low-cost maintenance.
keyChanges: Regius Ace introduced as HiAce twin for Japanese domestic market
LH series chassis with 2L (2.4L), 3L (2.8L) and 5L (3.0L) indirect diesel options
Distributor-ignition petrol engines available in select variants
Commercial van body styles prioritised for fleet and small business use
knownFor: Robust, simple engine designs favoured for commercial reliability
All diesel engines are interference design - timing belt critical
Injector pump wear common on high-mileage commercial use
Grey import availability to UK begins creating replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-1999 Toyota Regius Ace replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota Regius Ace LH113V engine replacement • Toyota Regius Ace 5L timing belt failure • Toyota Regius Ace 3RZ-FE distributor fault
enquiries: Toyota Regius Ace LH113V engine replacement
Toyota Regius Ace 5L timing belt failure

years.preview: LH Series Matures: 5L Diesel Dominates Commercial Demand
years.description: Late LH series Regius Ace vans consolidated around the 5L 3.0L diesel for commercial buyers, while timing belt awareness grew among UK grey import owners.
keyChanges: 5L 3.0L indirect diesel becomes dominant commercial powerplant
Minor facelifts and trim updates across LH series range
UK grey import market expands, increasing replacement engine demand
Timing belt failure awareness grows in owner forums
knownFor: 5L timing belt catastrophic failure risk if not replaced every 60k miles
Injector pump calibration drift causing smoke on high-mileage units
Strong parts compatibility with Toyota HiAce simplifies repairs
Commercial buyers prioritise minimal downtime over absolute lowest price
years.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace 2000-2004 engine replacement prices

years.preview: H200 Generation Launch: 1KD-FTV Common-Rail Turbo Arrives
years.description: The H200 generation introduced the powerful 1KD-FTV common-rail turbo diesel to Regius Ace, bringing improved performance but also the injector seal 'black death' issue that now drives UK replacement searches.
keyChanges: New H200 platform replaces LH series with modern common-rail diesel technology
1KD-FTV 3.0L turbo diesel becomes flagship commercial engine
Petrol options updated to 2TR-FE VVT-i 2.7L
KDH and TRH chassis codes introduced for diesel/petrol variants
knownFor: 1KD-FTV injector seal carbon buildup ('black death') emerges as dominant failure
VNT turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage commercial use
Improved power and efficiency over LH series engines
HiAce parts compatibility continues to simplify UK sourcing

years.preview: H200 Mid-Life: Engine Refinements & 1TR-FE Petrol Option

keyChanges: Minor updates to 1KD-FTV injection calibration and EGR systems
1TR-FE 2.0L VVT-i petrol introduced for urban commercial variants
Continued growth of UK grey import Regius Ace fleet

Rebuilt engine market matures with upgraded seal solutions for 1KD-FTV

faq.h2: Toyota Regius Ace Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Toyota Regius Ace engine in the UK?
items.answer: Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement costs range from £950 for a used LH series 2L diesel to £4,500 for a rebuilt H200 1KD-FTV D4D, depending on engine code, condition and whether you choose supply-only or supply & fit.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 1KD-FTV diesel: £1,800-£4,500 supply only
Used LH series 5L/3L: £650-£2,200 depending on mileage
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£950 typically
Prices vary by supplier location and engine warranty terms
items.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Toyota Regius Ace 1KD-FTV engine reliable?
items.answer: The 1KD-FTV is robust when maintained, but injector seal failure ('black death') at 80k-120k miles is common on high-mileage grey imports; rebuilt units with upgraded seals significantly improve long-term reliability.
keyPoints: Injector seal carbon buildup is the dominant failure mode
Timing chain design is generally reliable vs belt-driven LH engines
VNT turbo actuator and EGR valve issues occur at high mileage
Rebuilt engines with upgraded seals address the primary weakness
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 1KD-FTV Toyota Regius Ace engines with upgraded seals

items.question: What causes Toyota Regius Ace LH series engines to fail?
items.answer: LH series Regius Ace engines (2L/3L/5L) most commonly fail due to snapped timing belts on these interference engines, causing catastrophic valve and piston damage that requires full replacement.
keyPoints: All LH diesel engines are interference design - belt failure destroys engine
Timing belt must be replaced every 60k miles or 5 years regardless of appearance
Injector pump wear and glow plug failures also common at high mileage
Rebuilt engines include new belt kits fitted as standard
items.cta: Compare rebuilt LH series Toyota Regius Ace engine prices

items.question: Can I fit a Toyota HiAce engine in my Regius Ace?
items.answer: Yes - the Regius Ace shares engines and chassis with the Toyota HiAce, so 1KD-FTV, 5L, 2TR-FE and other codes are directly interchangeable between models, simplifying parts sourcing for UK grey import owners.
keyPoints: Regius Ace is a HiAce twin under different nameplate
Engine codes, mounts and wiring are compatible across models
HiAce parts availability in UK makes Regius Ace servicing easier
Always verify chassis code (KDH/TRH/LH) matches when sourcing
items.cta: Find Toyota Regius Ace engines with HiAce compatibility confirmed

items.question: What are the symptoms of 1KD-FTV injector seal failure on Regius Ace?
items.answer: Early signs include a distinct metallic ticking or tapping noise from the engine bay, especially when cold, often accompanied by a faint diesel smell and black carbon residue around injector bases.
keyPoints: Metallic ticking/tapping noise increasing with engine temperature
Visible black carbon 'soot' around injector bases ('black death')
Slight loss of power or rough idle as seals degrade
Diagnostic codes may show injector circuit or pressure faults
items.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace 1KD-FTV injector seal repair vs replacement costs

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Toyota Regius Ace 1KD-FTV engine?
items.answer: If injector seals are the only issue and caught early, seal replacement is cost-effective; if carbon has damaged the head/block or the engine has other high-mileage wear, a rebuilt unit offers better long-term value for commercial use.
keyPoints: Seal-only repair: £450-£850 if caught before head damage
Rebuilt engine: £1,800-£4,500 with upgraded seals and warranty
Commercial operators often prefer rebuilt for minimal downtime
Request compression test results before choosing repair path
items.cta: Get tailored quotes for your Toyota Regius Ace engine decision

items.question: How long does a Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 3-7 working days from UK specialists; supply & fit service usually completes the full replacement in 2-4 working days depending on workshop scheduling and vehicle accessibility.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 3-7 days for rebuilt units from UK network
Supply & fit labour: 1-2 days for straightforward H200/KDH variants
LH series may take longer if additional ancillaries need replacement
Commercial operators should book in advance to minimise downtime
items.cta: Find Toyota Regius Ace supply & fit specialists with fast turnaround

items.question: Do used Toyota Regius Ace engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Warranty terms vary by supplier and engine condition: used engines typically offer 3-6 months, rebuilt/reconditioned units include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranties as standard through EngineMarket's network.
keyPoints: Used engines: 3-6 months parts warranty typical
Rebuilt/reconditioned: minimum 12-month unlimited mileage standard
Always confirm warranty explicitly covers commercial vehicle use
Keep all paperwork and compression test reports for warranty claims
items.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Where can I buy a Toyota Regius Ace engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used Toyota Regius Ace engines, with options for supply-only or full supply & fit service nationwide.
keyPoints: Grey import specialists understand Regius Ace/HiAce compatibility
Rebuilt engines with upgraded seals available for 1KD-FTV units
Compare multiple quotes to find best value for your variant
All network suppliers offer minimum 12-month warranty on rebuilt units
items.cta: Get free quotes from trusted Toyota Regius Ace engine suppliers UK

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on an older Toyota Regius Ace?
items.answer: For commercial Regius Ace vans still generating revenue, a rebuilt engine with warranty often provides better value than repeated repairs or purchasing another grey import, especially if the chassis and body are in good condition.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost vs vehicle value: £1,800-£4,500 vs £3,000-£10,000 typical
Warranty protection reduces risk of repeat failure costs
Minimal downtime with supply & fit service preserves income
HiAce parts compatibility keeps ongoing maintenance affordable
items.cta: Compare Toyota Regius Ace engine replacement vs vehicle replacement costs
